MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Dec-07-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MAZDA MOTOR OF AMERICA, INC. DBA MAZDA NORTH'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to change venue to Sacramento County is granted.

In this lemon law case, plaintiff lives in Sacramento County, bought a Mazda in that county and had it repaired there. (Cmplt. 2:2-4; Ekblad Dec. Exs. C-D.) Defendant's headquarters is in Orange County. (Id. at Ex. E.) No activity occurred here in San Francisco.

Plaintiff's sole argument is that she could have had the Mazda repaired in San Francisco under her warranty (but did not). This, she says, allows San Francisco venue under CCP 395.5 as "a county where the contract is to be performed." However, plaintiff cites no authority holding that a county where a contract is, in the event, not performed is a proper venue. Plaintiff's theory would allow lemon law defendants to be vexed by cases venued hundreds of miles from any relevant activity. No sanctions are awarded to anyone.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Re-Notice Of Defendants Motion For Change Of Venue And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-03-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHELSEA D. HOLLINS, Esq. of Neale & Fhima APC (Tel: 949-661-1007) for plaintiff;
Atty. ERIK J. EKBLAD, Esq. of Beatty & Myers, LLP (Tel: 562-606-1530) for defendant Mazda Motor of America dba Mazda North American Operations.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MAZDA MOTOR OF AMERICA, INC. DBA MAZDA NORTH AMERICAN OPERATIONS's Motion For Change Of Venue And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's "motion for change of venue and request for monetary sanctions" is denied without prejudice.

A motion is required to state its basis. (CRC 3.1112(d).) Here, the motion states none of the five distinct statutes mentioned in defendant's memorandum, inappropriately leaving the court to try to guess the basis. (Mot. 1:24-2:6.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Oct-10-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ERIK J. EKBLAD, Esq. of Beatty & Myers, LLP (Tel: 562-606-1530) for defendant Mazda Motor of America, Inc. dba Mazda North American Operations;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MAZDA MOTOR OF AMERICA, INC. DBA MAZDA NORTH AMERICAN OPERATIONS'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

The Court reiterates that it has only been provided a courtesy copy of the reply papers for this matter.

The Court indicates that the parties are to contact this department by email with proposed agreed-upon new hearing dates for the moving party to re-notice this matter for, and the department will either approve the date or provide an alternative available hearing date. The parties should deliver their courtesy copies after a new hearing date has been approved/secured with this department.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is ordered off calendar so both parties can comply with Local Rule 2.7(B)(courtesy copies).

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
